title: Depth First Search (DFS) Traversal on a Graph
description: A visual representation of the DFS algorithm traversing a graph structure, highlighting the order of node visitation and the stack mechanism.
relationships: The algorithm starts at Node A and pushes it onto the stack. It then visits an adjacent unvisited node (e.g., B), marks it visited, and pushes it onto the stack. This process continues until a node with no unvisited neighbors is found, at which point the algorithm backtracks by popping nodes from the stack until it finds a node with an unvisited neighbor.
